understanding.goal_understanding: Complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level within its step budget, where solving a level means positioning every selectable piece so that all goal tiles marked '+' are occupied simultaneously (one selectable per goal) to trigger WIN and advance until the final level is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The controlled piece is the currently selected selectable indicated by player.id/player.position (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid), and movement actions shift it by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects. If a pushable object (e.g., block) is adjacent in the movement direction, the move may push it along with the selected piece if space permits. Walls ('#') generally prevent movement through them, though some boundaries can have non-uniform behavior so attempted moves are sometimes needed to confirm blocking; the movement.*_blocked flags are only hints and don’t guarantee failure. Levels can contain multiple selectables ('S'); using CLICK with an exact pixel/grid coordinate matching another selectable’s position should switch control to that piece, while clicking empty space or an invalid/blocked target has no effect.
